I always check my email in the morning.
Sofia usually sends her update on Friday.
I am never late.
David is always busy in the afternoon.
I always check my email at eight.
I usually reply in the morning.
I often talk to brokers.
I sometimes travel for work.
I never reply at night.
Good morning, Paula. How is your second week?
Busy. I always check my email at eight, and it is always full.
That is normal here. How often do you reply to the brokers?
I usually reply in the morning. Sometimes in the afternoon, if my schedule is full.
Good. And the update, how often do you send it?
I send it on Friday. Always. I am never late.
Sofia never asks twice, then. She only asks when the update is late.
David, can I ask you something? How often do you write in English?
Every day. And I always make mistakes. I send the email anyway. Nobody ever answers about my grammar. They answer about the account.
I usually stop and think too much. Not anymore.
How often do you send the update?
I usually reply in the morning, never at night.
I am never late with a deadline.
I sometimes travel for work. Not very often.

Hi, Paula. David here. Welcome to your second week. Let me tell you how our team works — the rhythm, not the rules. We always start the day with email. I check my inbox at eight, every morning. Sofia usually sends her update on Friday, at five — she is never late, and nobody here is. We visit brokers on Thursday. Not every Thursday — sometimes twice a month, sometimes more. And the reports go out on Friday. Always. One more thing, and this is the important one: I often write in English, and my English is not perfect. I always make mistakes, and I always send the email anyway. Nobody ever answered me about my grammar. They answer about the account. See you at nine. Goodbye.

Good morning, Paula. Sofia here. Two things about the rhythm of the commercial area, and then I let you work. One: I always want the update on Friday. Always. Not perfect — just on time. A short email is enough. Three lines: the account, the number, the next step. Two, and listen to this one carefully: you don't need to reply immediately. I never reply immediately. When my phone rings at seven in the evening, I don't answer it. I answer in the morning, and the world is still there. New people are often afraid of this. Don't be. In this team we are never late — but we are never in a hurry either. And one last thing, Paula. Your English. You sometimes stop in the middle of a sentence, and you think I notice. I don't. I notice that you understand every word I say. That is not a small thing — that is most of the job. See you on Friday.
